extraction.one_line_summary: IZAGIRRE Gorka attacked solo with 1.5km remaining from a 14-rider breakaway he'd policed all day, winning by 6 seconds over LASTRA.
tactical_patterns: IZAGIRRE Gorka chased every acceleration in 14-rider break before attacking, revealing he valued stage more than companions from kilometer 1.
O'CONNOR Ben attacked early in breakaway but exposed himself too soon, paying price on later climbs when others had reserves.
CRADDOCK Lawson bridged solo after hesitating riders wouldn't commit, showing back-to-back strong form signals genuine Grand Tour shape building.
Peloton let 14-rider break with O'CONNOR at only 37:08 GC go clear, showing top teams misjudged break's threat level.

rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Gorka: positive: Chased every move in breakaway then attacked with 1.5km left, revealing superior tactical patience and leg freshness management.
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: Attacked too early in breakaway and paid price later on climbs, exposing poor pacing judgment for rider hunting stage.
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Bridged solo to lead group and finished second after strong showing previous day, signaling genuine Grand Tour form building.
rider_signals.LASTRA Jonathan: positive: Finished second after being in final selection all day, showing consistent positioning and legs to contest finale.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Attacked late but caught by chasers with 1.5km left, mistimed final effort when IZAGIRRE still had reserves.
rider_signals.GHEBREIGZABHIER Amanuel: neutral: Dimension Data placed two riders in break with O'CONNOR but neither capitalized despite numerical advantage.
rider_signals.MADRAZO Ángel: negative: Leading KOM classification but lost contact on early acceleration, suggesting mountain points jersey not backed by genuine climbing legs.
